rationale_for_baseline_tier: An inflammatory back-pain flare of known axSpA with a normal neurological screen (no cauda equina or severe radiculopathy) can be managed with NSAIDs, frequent gentle movement and heat, with prompt GP follow-up — a T2 baseline. The mandatory escalation is for cauda equina/severe radiculopathy features, which are emergencies.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Loss of bladder/bowel control, saddle (groin/buttock) numbness, or bilateral leg weakness
escalation_edges.rationale: Cauda equina syndrome is a surgical emergency needing urgent decompression.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you lose control of your bladder or bowels, get numbness around your groin or back passage, or your legs become weak, call 000 or go to ED immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New shooting leg pain past the knee with numbness or progressive leg weakness
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests significant radiculopathy needing urgent assessment/imaging.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If pain starts shooting down your leg past the knee with numbness, or your leg becomes weak, see a doctor the same day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure he can mobilise safely to the bathroom; arrange help if needed.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a flare of your inflammatory back condition, not a slipped disc — your nerves are working normally. Take an anti-inflammatory with food, use heat, and move gently and often (about 2 minutes every hour) rather than resting completely. See your GP within a week. Call 000 or go to ED if you lose control of your bladder/bowels, get numbness around your groin, or your legs become weak.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1 week; 000/ED for cauda equina features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No after-hours care needed unless cauda equina/radiculopathy features develop.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of an inflammatory back-pain flare in nr-axSpA; cauda equina and severe radiculopathy screened negative; NSAIDs + movement-based advice given; cauda equina safety-net provided; rheumatology/therapy review recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring severe back pain without a cauda equina screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving cauda equina syndrome is missed; delayed decompression causes permanent incontinence and leg weakness.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for an inflammatory flare with a normal neuro screen is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot perform a formal neurological examination remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Screen cauda equina/radiculopathy by history, reassure if negative with a firm safety-net, and manage with movement-based advice.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either reassure without a cauda equina screen or over-triage to ED without red flags.
